title: Run `docker-compose up -d`; within 60 seconds the ministack, redis, langfuse ...
description: Run `docker-compose up -d`; within 60 seconds the ministack, redis, langfuse and hindsight containers report healthy. The init script `infra/localstack/init/01-create-resources.sh` has created the causeflow DynamoDB table, 4 SQS queues, 4 DLQs and the KMS alias `alias/causeflow-token-encryption`. A `curl http://localhost:4566/_ministack/health` returns 200.
steps: Step 1: Read the Acceptance Check AC-001 in project_specs.xml to understand the boundary it must satisfy.
Step 2: Exercise the boundary described in AC-001 (curl, CLI, dashboard click, or scripted call) against the running app and observe the response.

Step 3: If the observed response matches the acceptance description, set implementation=true (no code changes). If not, set implementation=false and capture the smallest possible diff to make it pass.

title: Run `pnpm dev`; within 10 seconds the Hono API is listening on PORT (default ...
description: Run `pnpm dev`; within 10 seconds the Hono API is listening on PORT (default 3099). A `curl http://localhost:3099/health` returns 200 with a JSON body listing {dynamodb: ok, redis: ok, sqs: ok, anthropic: ok} (Anthropic check is skipped when no key is configured).

title: Run `pnpm lint-invariants` (which calls `infra/scripts/check-invariants.ts`);...
description: Run `pnpm lint-invariants` (which calls `infra/scripts/check-invariants.ts`); the command exits 0. Manually introducing a cross-module direct function call (e.g. importing a use case from another module's `application/`) and re-running `pnpm lint-invariants` makes the command exit non-zero with a specific I1–I11 violation message.

title: After an incident is ingested, within 30 seconds the triage worker pulls the ...
description: After an incident is ingested, within 30 seconds the triage worker pulls the message, calls the Anthropic API with ANTHROPIC_TRIAGE_MODEL (default claude-sonnet-4-6), and updates the incident with a severity (one of critical|high|medium|low) and a suggested investigation mode. GET /api/v1/triage/:id returns the triaged incident with the severity set; GET /api/v1/triage lists triaged incidents for the tenant.

A trace is also recorded in the trace's `output` for the incident's audit chain.
